title: OCI Compute Instance
description: Schema for an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure compute instance. Defines the structure of a virtual machine or bare metal instance including its shape configuration, source image details, networking, lifecycle state, and metadata.

id.description: The OCID of the instance. Oracle Cloud Identifier (OCID) is a unique identifier assigned to every OCI resource.

compartmentId.description: The OCID of the compartment that contains the instance. Compartments are the primary building blocks for organizing cloud resources.

availabilityDomain.description: The availability domain the instance is running in. An availability domain is one or more data centers within a region.

faultDomain.description: The fault domain the instance is running in. Fault domains provide anti-affinity within an availability domain.

displayName.description: A user-friendly name for the instance. Does not have to be unique and is changeable. Avoid entering confidential information.

shape.description: The shape of the instance. The shape determines the number of CPUs, amount of memory, and other resources allocated to the instance.

region.description: The region that contains the availability domain the instance is running in.

imageId.description: Deprecated. Use sourceDetails instead. The OCID of the image used to boot the instance.

timeCreated.description: The date and time the instance was created, in RFC 3339 format.

metadata.description: Custom metadata key/value pairs provided for the instance. Common keys include ssh_authorized_keys for providing SSH public keys and user_data for cloud-init scripts (base64-encoded).

ssh_authorized_keys.description: One or more SSH public keys to be included in the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file for the default user on the instance. Separate multiple keys with newline characters.

user_data.description: Base64-encoded cloud-init user data script to run when the instance starts.

extendedMetadata.description: Additional metadata key/value pairs that can contain nested JSON objects.

launchMode.description: Specifies the configuration mode for launching VM instances.

timeMaintenanceRebootDue.description: The date and time the instance is expected to be stopped or migrated for infrastructure maintenance, if applicable.

capacityReservationId.description: The OCID of the compute capacity reservation this instance is launched under.

dedicatedVmHostId.description: The OCID of the dedicated virtual machine host the instance is placed on.

isCrossNumaNode.description: Whether the instance spans multiple NUMA nodes.

LifecycleState.description: The lifecycle state of a compute instance. The state transitions are: PROVISIONING -> RUNNING -> STOPPING -> STOPPED -> STARTING -> RUNNING, and RUNNING -> TERMINATING -> TERMINATED.

ShapeConfig.description: The shape configuration of an instance. For flexible shapes, this specifies the allocated OCPUs, memory, and other resources.

ocpus.description: The total number of OCPUs available to the instance.

memoryInGBs.description: The total amount of memory available to the instance, in gigabytes.

baselineOcpuUtilization.description: The baseline OCPU utilization for a subcore burstable instance. BASELINE_1_8 means 12.5% baseline, BASELINE_1_2 means 50% baseline, BASELINE_1_1 means 100% (non-burstable).

processorDescription.description: A short description of the instance processor.

networkingBandwidthInGbps.description: The networking bandwidth available to the instance, in gigabits per second.

maxVnicAttachments.description: The maximum number of VNIC attachments for the instance.

gpus.description: The number of GPUs available to the instance.

gpuDescription.description: A short description of the instance GPU.

localDisks.description: The number of local NVMe disks available to the instance.

localDisksTotalSizeInGBs.description: The aggregate size of all local disks, in gigabytes.

vcpus.description: The total number of VCPUs available. This is typically 2x the number of OCPUs.

SourceDetails.description: Details for the source used to launch the instance. The source is either an image or a boot volume.

sourceType.description: The source type for the instance.

imageId.description: The OCID of the image used to boot the instance. Required when sourceType is image.

bootVolumeId.description: The OCID of the boot volume used to boot the instance. Required when sourceType is bootVolume.

bootVolumeSizeInGBs.description: The size of the boot volume in GBs.

bootVolumeVpusPerGB.description: The number of volume performance units per GB for the boot volume. 10 = Balanced, 20 = Higher Performance, 30-120 = Ultra High Performance.

LaunchOptions.description: Options for tuning the compatibility and performance of VM shapes.

bootVolumeType.description: Emulation type for the boot volume attachment.

firmware.description: Firmware used to boot the VM.

networkType.description: Emulation type for the physical network interface card.

remoteDataVolumeType.description: Emulation type for remote data volume attachments.

isPvEncryptionInTransitEnabled.description: Whether in-transit encryption is enabled for the volume's paravirtualized attachment.

isConsistentVolumeNamingEnabled.description: Whether consistent volume naming is enabled.

AgentConfig.description: Configuration options for the Oracle Cloud Agent software running on the instance.

isMonitoringDisabled.description: Whether the Monitoring plugin is disabled.

isManagementDisabled.description: Whether the Management plugin is disabled.

areAllPluginsDisabled.description: Whether all Oracle Cloud Agent plugins are disabled.

pluginsConfig.description: The configuration of plugins associated with this instance.

PluginConfig.description: Configuration details for an Oracle Cloud Agent plugin.

desiredState.description: Whether the plugin should be enabled or disabled.

AvailabilityConfig.description: Options for VM migration during infrastructure maintenance events.

isLiveMigrationPreferred.description: Whether live migration is the preferred option for infrastructure maintenance.

recoveryAction.description: The lifecycle action to take when maintenance is due. RESTORE_INSTANCE restarts the instance after maintenance. STOP_INSTANCE keeps it stopped.

PlatformConfig.description: The platform configuration for the instance, including security settings like Secure Boot and TPM.

isSecureBootEnabled.description: Whether Secure Boot is enabled on the instance.

isTrustedPlatformModuleEnabled.description: Whether the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) is enabled.

isMeasuredBootEnabled.description: Whether the Measured Boot feature is enabled.

isMemoryEncryptionEnabled.description: Whether AMD Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) memory encryption is enabled.

DefinedTags.description: Defined tags for the resource. Each key is predefined and scoped to a namespace. Format: {namespace: {key: value}}.

FreeformTags.description: Free-form tags for the resource. Each tag is a simple key-value pair with no predefined name, type, or namespace.

Shape.description: A compute shape determines the resources allocated to an instance. Shapes can be fixed (predetermined resources) or flexible (configurable OCPUs and memory).

availabilityDomain.description: The availability domain where the shape is available. Null if available across all ADs.

ocpus.description: The default number of OCPUs for this shape.

memoryInGBs.description: The default amount of memory for this shape, in GB.

processorDescription.description: A short description of the processor.

networkingBandwidthInGbps.description: The networking bandwidth for this shape, in Gbps.

maxVnicAttachments.description: The maximum number of VNICs that can be attached.

gpus.description: The number of GPUs for this shape.

gpuDescription.description: A short description of the GPU.

localDisks.description: The number of local disks for this shape.

localDisksTotalSizeInGBs.description: The aggregate local disk size in GB.

isFlexible.description: Whether the shape supports flexible OCPU and memory configuration.

isBilledForStoppedInstance.description: Whether billing continues when the instance is stopped.

Image.description: A boot disk image for launching compute instances. Images can be Oracle-provided platform images, custom images created from instances, or Marketplace partner images.

compartmentId.description: The OCID of the compartment containing the image.

displayName.description: A user-friendly name for the image.

operatingSystem.description: The operating system of the image.

lifecycleState.description: The lifecycle state of the image.

timeCreated.description: The date and time the image was created.

baseImageId.description: The OCID of the image originally used to create this custom image.

sizeInMBs.description: The boot volume size for instances launched from this image, in MBs.

billableSizeInGBs.description: The billable size of the image in GBs.

createImageAllowed.description: Whether instances from this image can create new images.

launchMode.description: The launch mode of instances created from this image.
